items.name: Africa
facts: Africa is the second-largest continent.
The Sahara Desert stretches across part of northern Africa.
Algeria is the largest country on this continent by land area.
Lagos is one of the major cities learners often connect with this continent.
funFacts: This is one of the seven large land areas on Earth.
54 is the approximate number of countries students usually learn for it.
A common clue linked to it is desert.
clues: It is the second-largest continent.
The Sahara Desert stretches across much of its north.
It has about 54 countries.
Algeria is the largest country there by area.
Lagos is one major city often linked with it.
The Equator runs across the middle of it.
Many famous savannas and wildlife parks are there.
The Nile River is one of its best-known landmarks.
Madagascar lies just off its southeastern coast.
It spans land both north and south of the Equator.

items.name: Antarctica
facts: Antarctica is the coldest continent on Earth.
It is covered mostly by thick ice.
No countries are located there.
It does not have permanent cities.
funFacts: This is one of the seven large land areas on Earth.
Scientists use research stations there instead of permanent cities.
A common clue linked to it is ice.
clues: It is the coldest continent on Earth.
Most of it is covered by thick ice.
It surrounds the South Pole.
It has no countries.
It has no permanent cities.
Scientists work there in research stations.
Penguins and seals are easier to find there than people.
It is the driest and windiest continent.
It is entirely in the Southern Hemisphere.
A huge amount of Earth's ice is stored there.

items.name: Asia
facts: Asia is the largest continent by area and population.
Mount Everest stands on the border of Nepal and China in Asia.
China is the largest country on this continent by land area.
Tokyo is one of the major cities learners often connect with this continent.
funFacts: This is one of the seven large land areas on Earth.
49 is the approximate number of countries students usually learn for it.
A common clue linked to it is mountains.
clues: It is the largest continent by area and population.
Mount Everest sits on its southern edge.
It has about 49 countries.
China is the largest country there by area.
Tokyo is one major city often linked with it.
It stretches from the Middle East to the Pacific Ocean.
India, Japan, and Indonesia are all part of it.
It is mostly in the Northern Hemisphere.
Many major religions began there.
It includes deserts, rainforests, and very high mountain ranges.

items.name: Europe
facts: Europe has many countries close together.
Many famous castles and old cities are in Europe.
Ukraine is the largest country on this continent by land area.
Istanbul is one of the major cities learners often connect with this continent.
funFacts: This is one of the seven large land areas on Earth.
44 is the approximate number of countries students usually learn for it.
A common clue linked to it is history.
clues: Many countries sit close together there.

It has about 44 countries.

Istanbul is one major city often linked with it.
Many famous castles, cathedrals, and old capitals are there.
The Alps cross part of it.
France, Germany, Italy, and Spain are all on it.
It is often taught as the continent west of Asia.
Peninsulas and coastlines shape much of its map.

items.name: North America
facts: North America includes Canada, the United States, and Mexico.
It has mountains, deserts, forests, and coastlines.
Canada is the largest country on this continent by land area.
Mexico City is one of the major cities learners often connect with this continent.
funFacts: This is one of the seven large land areas on Earth.
23 is the approximate number of countries students usually learn for it.
A common clue linked to it is varied.

It stretches from the Arctic toward the Caribbean.

Mexico City is one major city often linked with it.
The Rocky Mountains run through part of it.
The Great Lakes are one of its best-known features.
It includes Greenland and many Caribbean islands.
It connects to the next continent by a narrow land bridge in the south.

items.name: South America
facts: The Amazon Rainforest covers part of South America.
The Andes are one of the longest mountain ranges in the world.
Brazil is the largest country on this continent by land area.
Sao Paulo is one of the major cities learners often connect with this continent.

12 is the approximate number of countries students usually learn for it.
A common clue linked to it is rainforest.

Sao Paulo is one major city often linked with it.

It has both Pacific and Atlantic coastlines.
It begins just south of the narrow land bridge at Panama.

items.name: Australia
facts: Australia is the smallest continent.
It is famous for unique animals like kangaroos and koalas.
Australia is the largest country on this continent by land area.
Sydney is one of the major cities learners often connect with this continent.
